title: inspect template for .NetworkSettings.Ports inconsistent with docker
body:
### Issue Description

kind-load-balancer (part of github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cloud-provider-kind) fails to start with the error:

`"Failed to get external kubeconfig" err="failed to get api server port: command "docker inspect --format '{{ with (index (index .NetworkSettings.Ports "6443/tcp") 0) }}{{ printf "%s\t%s" .HostIp .HostPort }}{{ end }}' kind-control-plane" failed with error: exit status 125" cluster="kind"`

The inspect works under docker, but fails under podman with the docker compatibility command.

Attempting to iterate over the object shows the differences between docker and podman:

moby-engine docker:
```
$ docker inspect --format '{{ range $k, $v := (index .NetworkSettings.Ports "6443/tcp") }}{{ print $k " -> " $v "\n" }}{{end}}' kind-control-plane
0 -> map[HostIp:127.0.0.1 HostPort:4400]

$ docker inspect --format '{{ range $k, $v := (index (index .NetworkSettings.Ports "6443/tcp") 0) }}{{ print $k " -> " $v "\n" }}{{end}}' kind-control-plane
HostIp -> 127.0.0.1
HostPort -> 4400

$ docker inspect --format '{{ with (index (index .NetworkSettings.Ports "6443/tcp") 0) }}{{ printf "%s\t%s" .HostIp .HostPort }}{{ end }}' kind-control-plane
127.0.0.1	4400
```

podman docker:
```
$ docker inspect --format '{{ range $k, $v := (index .NetworkSettings.Ports "6443/tcp") }}{{ print $k " -> " $v "\n" }}{{end}}' kind-control-plane
0 -> {127.0.0.1 44481}

$ docker inspect --format '{{ range $k, $v := (index (index .NetworkSettings.Ports "6443/tcp") 0) }}{{ print $k " -> " $v "\n" }}{{end}}' kind-control-plane
Error: template: inspect:1:79: executing "inspect" at <0>: range can't iterate over {127.0.0.1 44481}

$ docker inspect --format '{{ with (index (index .NetworkSettings.Ports "6443/tcp") 0) }}{{ printf "%s\t%s" .HostIp .HostPort }}{{ end }}' kind-control-plane
Error: template: inspect:1:91: executing "inspect" at <.HostIp>: can't evaluate field HostIp in type define.InspectHostPort
```

As visible above, docker exposes NetworkSettings.Ports as a array of maps, but podman as an array of objects.  As a workaround, the values can be accessed in podman using the object definition of define.InspectHostPort with:
```
$ docker inspect --format '{{ with (index (index .NetworkSettings.Ports "6443/tcp") 0) }}{{ printf "%s\t%s" .HostIP .HostPort }}{{ end }}' kind-control-plane
127.0.0.1	44481
```

but the same command in moby-engine fails:
```
$ docker inspect --format '{{ with (index (index .NetworkSettings.Ports "6443/tcp") 0) }}{{ printf "%s\t%s" .HostIP .HostPort }}{{ end }}' kind-control-plane
template parsing error: template: :1:81: executing "" at <.HostIP>: map has no entry for key "HostIP"
```




### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman docker compatibility
2. Start a container `docker run --rm -d --name test --publish=127.0.0.1:4400:6443/tcp alpine sleep infinity`
3. Run: `docker inspect --format '{{ with (index (index .NetworkSettings.Ports "6443/tcp") 0) }}{{ printf "%s\t%s" .HostIp .HostPort }}{{ end }}' test`


### Describe the results you received

Error: template: inspect:1:91: executing "inspect" at <.HostIp>: can't evaluate field HostIp in type define.InspectHostPort


### Describe the results you expected

127.0.0.1	4400


### podman info output

comments.body:
The `{{.HostIp}}` access has been fixed by adding a `HostIp()` method to `InspectHostPort`.

The range over individual port bindings (struct vs map) cannot be fixed without a breaking API change, which would require `podman 7`.

If the `.HostIp` fix resolves the original cloud-provider-kind failure, please close this issue.

@Honny1 - I tried a build at commit [030b0be](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/commit/030b0be9d824ce9e6fb56acfbaa2af081a9e2973) and using the issue testcase, still got an error: 
```
podman inspect --format '{{ (index (index .NetworkSettings.Ports "6443/tcp") 0).HostIp }}' test
Error: template: inspect:1:63: executing "inspect" at <0>: can't evaluate field HostIp in type define.InspectHostPort
```

which appears to be the e2e test added in the same commit... but then again I may have done something wrong with my mock build :). Should the build at that commit work?
